prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for nitrobenzene (14 atoms: 2×O, 1×N, 6×C, 5×H) at DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1, using PySCF via the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill. Partial charges computed via Mulliken scheme. N±1 states used doublet (multiplicity 2) at ±1 charge. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, not re-optimized at this level. Both f+ and f- sum to 1.0000 (charge conservation satisfied). integrity.trustworthy = true, status = ok. No warnings reported. Headline value: most electrophilic site f+ = 0.49641782 at atom 4 (C, ipso carbon attached to –NO₂). Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C, ortho carbon), f– = 0.10728820. The two oxygen atoms of the nitro group also show large f+ (~0.473 each). Full per-atom Fukui indices (f+, f–, f0, dual descriptor) reported in table above.
